subject: Re: WarpTools ts_template_match on multiple GPUs
body:
Hi Juha,
Template matching is very good at making full use of the GPU, there is no benefit to running multiple processes per GPU for this program. I haven’t checked, but maybe Dimitry added some code which explicitly disallows running it in this less efficient way.
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Dec 19, 2024, at 22:24, Juha Huiskonen <juha.huiskonen@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿Hi all,
When I try to use multiple GPUs (or processes per GPU) to run a template matching on tomogram, I get only one worker (and one process). This is the command I tried:
WarpTools ts_template_match \
--settings warp_tiltseries.settings \
--tomo_angpix 10 \
--subdivisions 5 \
--template_path templates/postprocess.mrc \
--template_angpix 3.09 \
--template_diameter 900 \
--peak_distance 100 \
--npeaks 5000 \
--symmetry C1 \
--whiten \
--subvolume_size 192 \
--perdevice 1
However, only GPU 0 is active.  The memory consumption is the same as with --perdevice 1 and I can see only one process running. 
Does WarpTools ts_template_match support multiple GPUs? I have a high sub-divisions parameter, as I have a long filament segment, and trying to make the segments meet accurately for visualization. 
Cheers,
Juha




--
